data.title: record-review.sh: overlap check fails when reviewer-findings.json written to different artifacts dir than session
data.description:
## Incident Overview
When code-reviewer sub-agents are dispatched from the orchestrator context during per-worktree review-commit, they write reviewer-findings.json to the session's artifacts dir (keyed on orchestrator REPO_ROOT hash) rather than the worktree's artifacts dir (keyed on worktree REPO_ROOT hash). record-review.sh then fails the overlap check because the findings reference files not in the worktree's staged diff.

## Reproduction
1. Enable worktree.isolation_enabled=true
2. Dispatch a code-reviewer sub-agent from the orchestrator context targeting a worktree's diff
3. The reviewer writes findings to get_artifacts_dir() which resolves to the orchestrator's artifacts dir
4. record-review.sh --findings-file pointing to the correct file still fails overlap because CHANGED_FILES is computed from the worktree's git state

## Expected Behavior
Reviewer findings should be written to the same artifacts dir that record-review.sh reads from, or record-review.sh should accept findings from any artifacts dir without overlap mismatch.

## Observed Behavior
Multiple retry cycles required per worktree merge to get review recorded. Workaround: use --findings-file flag and manually ensure hash consistency.
